meta.title: The ULTIMATE Case for Sola Scriptura
meta.author: Gavin Ortlund

questions.question: According to Ortlund, sola scriptura most fundamentally claims that:
options: Scripture is the only infallible rule for the church
Scripture is the only authority in the church
Only doctrines explicitly stated in the Bible may be believed
Tradition has no binding authority whatsoever
questions.answer: Scripture is the only infallible rule for the church

questions.question: In the video, what is the main line of disagreement with Roman Catholic and Eastern Orthodox interlocutors?
options: Whether scripture is without error
Whether the word "sola" applies—i.e., whether anything besides scripture is also an infallible rule
Whether the Old Testament is inspired
Whether councils ever occurred in church history
questions.answer: Whether the word "sola" applies—i.e., whether anything besides scripture is also an infallible rule

questions.question: Ortlund uses a baseball umpire to illustrate that:
options: Infallibility and authority are the same thing
Protestants reject all human judgment in the church
Someone can have real authority to make binding calls yet still be fallible
Only sporting events have clear rules
questions.answer: Someone can have real authority to make binding calls yet still be fallible

questions.question: Article 20 of the Thirty-Nine Articles is cited to show that historic Protestantism:
options: Denied that the church has any authority in controversies of faith
Required every ceremony to be explicitly biblical
Rejected the Old Testament as a rule of faith
Affirmed the church has authority in controversies of faith while distinguishing that from infallibility
questions.answer: Affirmed the church has authority in controversies of faith while distinguishing that from infallibility

questions.question: Francis Turretin is quoted as distinguishing:
options: Scripture as supreme judge and the church as subordinate judge
Scripture and tradition as equally infallible
The Old and New Testaments as unrelated authorities
Faith and works as identical grounds of justification
questions.answer: Scripture as supreme judge and the church as subordinate judge

questions.question: Ortlund says the doctrine often confused with sola scriptura—sufficiency of scripture—is:
options: Identical to sola scriptura in every tradition
A related but distinct doctrine articulated with different emphases across Protestant traditions
Rejected by the Westminster Confession
Only taught by Eastern Orthodoxy
questions.answer: A related but distinct doctrine articulated with different emphases across Protestant traditions

questions.question: The Westminster Confession language Ortlund highlights allows doctrine to be known not only expressly in scripture but also:
options: By majority vote of any local congregation
Only by papal definition
By good and necessary consequence deduced from scripture
Solely through unwritten apostolic tradition independent of the text
questions.answer: By good and necessary consequence deduced from scripture

questions.question: On 2 Timothy 3:16 (theopneustos), Ortlund follows Stott in saying "God-breathed" means:
options: God adds inspiration to a text that was originally merely human
Only the reader is inspired when reading
Paul was speaking only of the Septuagint translators
The scripture itself—its words—is what is breathed out by God
questions.answer: The scripture itself—its words—is what is breathed out by God

questions.question: B. B. Warfield’s discussion of 2 Peter 1:21 is used to stress that "carried along" implies:
options: More than vague guidance—the human authors’ words are from God
Authors had general spiritual feelings but free invention of content
Only the original audiences were inspired
Tradition and scripture are the same ontologically
questions.answer: More than vague guidance—the human authors’ words are from God

questions.question: Doug Moo is cited regarding Romans 3:2 and "oracles of God" as likely referring to:
options: The rabbinic oral law alone
The Old Testament as a whole
Only the Psalms
Imperial Roman edicts
questions.answer: The Old Testament as a whole

questions.question: Ortlund cites Matthew 19 as significant because Jesus treats Old Testament scripture as:
options: Merely human literature with errors
Less authoritative than Pharisaic tradition
God speaking—not only narratives in which God speaks
Irrelevant to ethical questions
questions.answer: God speaking—not only narratives in which God speaks

questions.question: Ortlund argues Roman Catholic theology still distinguishes papal ex cathedra teaching from scripture because ex cathedra is:
options: The same as new God-breathed revelation added to the apostolic deposit
Always fallible while scripture is not
Only about discipline, never doctrine
Infallible interpretation but not the same as spirit-carried divine speech like scripture
questions.answer: Infallible interpretation but not the same as spirit-carried divine speech like scripture

questions.question: In the Matthew 15 / Mark 7 discussion, Ortlund notes the Pharisees sat on Moses’ seat, which shows:
options: They had legitimate God-given teaching authority yet still could be corrected by scripture over tradition
They had no real authority at all
Jesus denied any role for teachers in Israel
Oral law was impossible before the first century
questions.answer: They had legitimate God-given teaching authority yet still could be corrected by scripture over tradition

questions.question: Ortlund connects Mark 7:13 (“making void the word of God”) to which Old Testament passages Jesus had just cited?
options: Genesis 1–2 only
Exodus 20 and Exodus 21:7
Isaiah 53 alone
The genealogies in 1 Chronicles
questions.answer: Exodus 20 and Exodus 21:7

questions.question: The Bereans in Acts 17:11 are praised for:
options: Rejecting all oral preaching
Establishing papal infallibility
Examining the scriptures daily to test Paul’s message
Ignoring the Jewish Scriptures
questions.answer: Examining the scriptures daily to test Paul’s message

questions.question: Galatians 1:8 is used to argue that even _____ should be tested against the deposit of revelation already given.
options: Roman governors
Psalm singers only
The Septuagint
Angels
questions.answer: Angels

questions.question: Ortlund’s main structural argument for sola scriptura combines which two considerations?
options: Scripture’s nature (what it is) and scripture’s role among God’s people (what it does)
Church growth statistics and liturgical aesthetics
Political history and archaeology only
The number of sacraments and Marian feasts
questions.answer: Scripture’s nature (what it is) and scripture’s role among God’s people (what it does)

questions.question: The "telephone game" analogy is applied primarily to:
options: Literary copying of biblical manuscripts across centuries
Fallible transmission of claimed oral apostolic tradition across generations
Jesus’ parables in the Synoptic Gospels
Translation from Hebrew to Greek only
questions.answer: Fallible transmission of claimed oral apostolic tradition across generations

questions.question: The second-century dispute over the date of Easter is used to show that:
options: All bishops agreed immediately on every detail
Scripture was irrelevant to early Christians
Both sides appealed to apostolic tradition yet disagreed on a factual matter
The Council of Nicaea invented the New Testament canon
questions.answer: Both sides appealed to apostolic tradition yet disagreed on a factual matter

questions.question: Irenaeus is mentioned as an example of early appeal to apostolic tradition for:
options: The date of Christmas in December
Denying the resurrection
Rejecting the Old Testament
A view of Jesus dying as a middle-aged man that hardly anyone affirms today
questions.answer: A view of Jesus dying as a middle-aged man that hardly anyone affirms today

questions.question: Which is NOT one of Ortlund’s four challenges to post-apostolic ecclesial infallibility?
options: It is clearly taught on every page of the pastoral epistles as a post-apostolic charism
It has no good precedent in the Old Testament
It is not clearly envisioned for the post-apostolic church in the New Testament
Once it arises historically, it seems to create confusion (e.g., shifting interpretations over centuries)
questions.answer: It is clearly taught on every page of the pastoral epistles as a post-apostolic charism

questions.question: Ortlund argues Matthew 18 language about binding and loosing is, in his reading, about:
options: Guaranteeing every medieval council was infallible
The apostles, not a proof text for an ongoing infallible magisterium centuries later
Replacing scripture with community consensus
Civil tax courts in Galilee
questions.answer: The apostles, not a proof text for an ongoing infallible magisterium centuries later

questions.question: The Sanhedrin is brought up to illustrate that:
options: Israel had an infallible supreme court in the Old Testament
The Talmud rejects all sacrifice
Jewish tradition could envision erroneous court rulings—fallibility, not an ongoing infallible adjudicator of revelation
Jesus denied Moses’ authority
questions.answer: Jewish tradition could envision erroneous court rulings—fallibility, not an ongoing infallible adjudicator of revelation

questions.question: Ortlund compares the conclusion "scripture is the only infallible rule" to:
options: Proving the Trinity without the New Testament
Denying that the church can ever make decisions
Equating all religions’ sacred texts
Saying Earth is the only planet in our solar system with life if you grant life here and deny it elsewhere
questions.answer: Saying Earth is the only planet in our solar system with life if you grant life here and deny it elsewhere

questions.question: At the end of the video, Ortlund directs viewers with canon concerns to:
options: A separate dedicated video he has made on the topic
Ignore the issue entirely
The Council of Trent only
A debate with Trent Horn on icons
questions.answer: A separate dedicated video he has made on the topic
